sections.summary: Good Status applies from CGPA 2.00 and above. Academic Probation may apply when CGPA is below 2.00. Academic Dismissal may apply if CGPA remains below 2.00 for three consecutive semesters.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for probation, dismissal, and CGPA standing questions.
sections.safety_note: Do not conclude dismissal from one CGPA value alone. Consecutive semester pattern must also be considered.

sections.summary: The handbook defines the official grade ranges and grade points, including A+/A = 4.00 down to F = 0.00.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for GPA/CGPA explanation and grade-point interpretation.
sections.safety_note: If the user asks for calculation, require courses, credits, and grades.

sections.summary: Students may add courses without penalty up to the end of the first two weeks of a normal semester or the first week of a short semester. Late registration may be allowed until the end of the fourth week of a normal semester or second week of a short semester with approval and penalty.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for questions about changing registered courses early in the semester.
sections.safety_note: Do not extend deadlines beyond what is stated in the handbook.

sections.summary: Course withdrawal is allowed after the add/drop period but before the thirteenth week of the normal semester or the seventh week of the short semester. A W grade appears on the transcript and does not count toward GPA.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for subject-level withdrawal questions.
sections.safety_note: No withdrawal is permitted after the deadline except in extraordinary circumstances stated by the institution.

sections.summary: Students may withdraw completely from the University before the thirteenth week of the normal semester or the seventh week of the short semester, subject to the required clearances and official process.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for complete withdrawal from the University, not a single course.
sections.safety_note: Withdrawal is not official until the required form and clearances are completed.

sections.summary: Students may apply for postponement of studies through the Academic Office, with approval by the Dean and Vice President Academic. Postponement is not allowed after the twelfth week of the long semester or the sixth week of the short semester.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for deferment/postponement of studies.
sections.safety_note: Do not advise postponement for the purpose of studying elsewhere unless the handbook explicitly permits it.

sections.summary: Registration for new students is 26 November 2025, first day of lecture is 01 December 2025, last day for add/drop is 12 December 2025, and final examinations run from 26 March 2026 to 05 April 2026.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for November 2025 intake date questions.
sections.safety_note: Important dates are semester-specific and should not be generalized to other intakes.

sections.reference: ACE305 – Bachelor of Civil Engineering with Honours
sections.summary: Bachelor of Civil Engineering with Honours is mapped at 140 total credit hours in the November dataset.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for Civil Engineering programme and credit questions.
sections.safety_note: Compulsory components should still be checked before final graduation confirmation.

sections.summary: Bachelor in Software Engineering (Honours) is mapped at 120 total credit hours. Industrial Training is 6 credits, and the prerequisite requires completed 86 credit hours.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for Software Engineering duration, credits, and graduation checks.
sections.safety_note: Graduation still depends on compulsory component completion, not credits alone.

sections.summary: Bachelor in Computer Science (Honours) is mapped at 120 total credit hours in the November intake dataset.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for Computer Science programme and graduation queries.
sections.safety_note: If the user requests fine-grained curriculum breakdown, refer to the programme structure.

sections.summary: Diploma in Information Technology is mapped at 92 total credit hours and 2 years 6 months in the November dataset.
sections.response_hint: Use this section for DIT programme and graduation checks.
sections.safety_note: Where finer detail is required, confirm against the official academic structure.
